reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement precisely. Polynomials f and g have rational coefficients (Polynomial ℚ). The hypothesis `∀ n : ℕ, ∃ a : ℤ, (f * g).coeff n = a` states that every coefficient of f·g is (equal to the ℤ→ℚ coercion of) an integer — exactly "f(x)g(x) has integer coefficients". Indices i, j are taken as parameters before the hypothesis, giving effective universal quantification over "any coefficient of f" and "any coefficient of g". The conclusion `∃ a : ℤ, f.coeff i * g.coeff j = a` asserts that the product is an integer (via the standard ℤ→ℚ coercion pattern). The quantifier structure, types, hypothesis, and conclusion all align with the informal claim.
